title: Time Management & Productivity
source: Harvard Business Review, APA, National Sleep Foundation

chunks.title: Pomodoro Technique & Focus Intervals
chunks.content: The Pomodoro Technique (Francesco Cirillo, 1980s): Work in 25-minute focused intervals, then take a 5-minute break. After 4 intervals (2 hours), take a 15-30 minute break. Research shows this reduces mental fatigue and improves sustained attention. Variations: 50/10 for deep work, 90/20 for creative work (matching ultradian rhythms). Key: eliminate all distractions during focus intervals. Source: Cirillo (2006), APA research on sustained attention.

chunks.title: Optimal Morning Routine for Productivity
chunks.content: Research-backed morning routine: 1) Wake at consistent time (±30 min, even weekends) — stabilises circadian rhythm. 2) Get 5-10 min sunlight within 30 min of waking — resets cortisol cycle (Huberman Lab). 3) Hydrate: 500ml water upon waking. 4) Delay caffeine 90 min after waking (adenosine clearance). 5) Do most cognitively demanding task in first 2-3 hours (cortisol peak). 6) Avoid phone/email for first 30 min (reduces reactive mindset). Source: Sleep Foundation, Huberman Lab, Harvard Business Review.

chunks.title: Task Batching & Context Switching Costs
chunks.content: Context switching between tasks costs 15-25 minutes of recovery time per switch (University of California, Irvine). Solution: Batch similar tasks together. Email: check 2-3 times/day at set times, not continuously. Meetings: batch into 1-2 blocks per day. Deep work: schedule 2-4 hour uninterrupted blocks. Admin tasks: batch into one session. Key principle: 'Maker's Schedule vs Manager's Schedule' (Paul Graham) — creators need long uninterrupted blocks, managers work in 30-min slots.

chunks.title: Energy Management Throughout the Day
chunks.content: Typical energy/alertness curve: Peak 1: 9-11 AM (cortisol high, ideal for analytical work). Dip: 1-3 PM (post-lunch circadian dip — do routine/admin tasks). Peak 2: 4-6 PM (second wind, good for creative work). Evening decline: After 7 PM (wind down, planning for next day). Tips: Eat light lunch to reduce afternoon dip. 10-20 min nap or walk at 1-2 PM boosts afternoon performance by 34% (NASA study). Plan hardest tasks during energy peaks. Source: National Sleep Foundation, NASA fatigue study.
